message:
fix(net): use injected time source in happy eyeballs and fix DNS test parameters

RaceConnections now constructs its timeout Sleep with the injected
time_getter function via Sleep::with_time_getter instead of Sleep::new,
ensuring the timeout fires based on the same clock domain as the
connection attempts. Previously, the sleep used wall_now() even when
the resolver was configured with a test clock, causing timeout behavior
to depend on real elapsed time rather than the injected time.

DNS resolver test for happy eyeballs timeout updated:
- Timeout increased from Duration::ZERO to 5 seconds so the first poll
  returns Pending (the timeout is not yet due) rather than immediately
  timing out
- Test IPs changed from loopback (::1, 127.0.0.1) to documentation-range
  addresses (2001:db8::1, 198.51.100.1) that are guaranteed unroutable,
  preventing accidental connection success on the test host
- Time advance increased from 1s to 15s to exceed the 5s timeout and
  trigger the expected DnsError::Timeout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
